title: Cisco OSPF Single-Area
description: Enable OSPF in a single area, assign a router ID, advertise networks, configure passive interfaces, and originate a default route into OSPF.

tasks.prompt: Enter Global Configuration mode to configure OSPF.
tasks.explanation: OSPF process configuration begins in global configuration mode.
The 'router ospf [process-id]' command enters router configuration mode. The process ID is locally significant.

tasks.prompt: Manually set the OSPF router ID to 1.1.1.1.
tasks.explanation: The router ID uniquely identifies the OSPF router. A loopback address is commonly used for stability.

tasks.prompt: Advertise all 10.x.x.x interfaces into OSPF area 0 using wildcard mask 0.255.255.255.
tasks.explanation: The network command enables OSPF on interfaces whose addresses fall within the specified range and assigns them to an area.

tasks.prompt: Advertise the 192.168.1.0/24 LAN segment into OSPF area 0.
tasks.explanation: Each network statement uses a wildcard mask (inverse of the subnet mask) to match interfaces for OSPF participation.

tasks.prompt: Set all interfaces to passive by default so they do not send OSPF hellos.
tasks.explanation: Passive interfaces still advertise their subnets in LSAs but suppress OSPF neighbor adjacencies on those interfaces.

tasks.prompt: Allow OSPF adjacencies on GigabitEthernet0/0 by removing it from the passive list.
tasks.explanation: After enabling passive-interface default, explicitly exempt interfaces that must form OSPF neighbor relationships.

tasks.prompt: Explicitly mark Loopback0 as a passive interface.
tasks.explanation: Loopback interfaces never need to form adjacencies; marking them passive is a best practice even without passive-interface default.

tasks.prompt: Advertise this router's default route into the OSPF domain.
tasks.explanation: An ASBR uses this command to inject a default route into OSPF, but the router must already have a default route installed.

tasks.prompt: Originate a default route into OSPF even when no default route exists in the routing table.
tasks.explanation: The 'always' keyword forces default route advertisement regardless of whether a default route is present locally.

tasks.prompt: Set the OSPF reference bandwidth to 10000 Mbps for accurate cost calculation on Gigabit links.
tasks.explanation: OSPF cost is reference-bandwidth divided by interface bandwidth. Raising the reference value lowers costs on fast interfaces.
This command lists adjacent OSPF routers and their states. FULL/DR or FULL/BDR indicates a healthy adjacency.
